assumptions: The developer has a supported browser and access to two devices that can install Tailscale clients.
No tailnet or enrolled devices exist yet.
No identity-provider choice or OS install path has been fixed.

developer_goal: Create a Tailscale account and a private tailnet, then enroll devices until the admin console is reachable with the quickstart's first-success state.

first_success.outcome: A new tailnet contains two authenticated devices and the developer reaches the Tailscale admin console.
first_success.observable_signal: After the second device authenticates, both devices display; selecting Take me home opens the admin console.

route_selection.surface: Tailscale web Get Started onboarding and client installers
route_selection.rule: Prefer the documented Create a tailnet quickstart; do not invent a winner among identity providers or OS installers when those choices change required actions.

candidates.name: Web Get Started then install two clients
candidates.reason: This is the primary documented sequence, but SSO provider and OS install steps still branch the action count.

candidates.name: Install client first then sign up
candidates.reason: The quickstart explicitly allows downloading and installing the client before signup, which reorders account creation versus install.

candidates.name: Public-domain Personal plan versus custom-domain Enterprise trial signup
candidates.reason: Official notes say the identity-provider email domain auto-enrolls Personal versus a 14-day Enterprise trial, a consequential entitlement fork.

required_path.action: Open Get Started and sign up with a supported SSO identity provider.

required_fields.label: Identity provider account
required_fields.notes: Exact IdP chooser labels and any secondary IdP consent fields are not fully enumerated on the public quickstart page.

required_path.observable_result: Tailscale creates a tailnet and shows the Welcome to Tailscale page.

required_path.action: Select Business use or Personal use.
required_path.observable_result: Tailscale advances to the first-device enrollment step.

required_path.action: Select the OS for the first device, install the Tailscale client, and authenticate with the same credentials used at signup.
required_path.observable_result: The first device appears in the browser onboarding window after OS-specific install completes.

required_path.action: Select the OS for a second device, share the install link, install the client, and authenticate the second device.
required_path.observable_result: Both devices display in the onboarding UI.

prerequisites.description: Access to two devices capable of installing a Tailscale client is required by the official Create a tailnet sequence.
A supported SSO identity provider account is required to sign up.

external_gates.description: SSO identity-provider authorization is required to create the tailnet.
Signup email domain determines Personal plan versus custom-domain Enterprise trial enrollment.

platform_outcomes.description: Tailscale creates the tailnet after SSO signup.
Authenticated devices appear in onboarding and later in the Machines view of the admin console.

excluded.item: Renaming devices, inviting users, and optional MagicDNS configuration
excluded.reason: These appear after Take me home in the quickstart and are not required for the documented two-device terminal.

excluded.item: Reading the under-10-minute marketing video as a measured action
excluded.reason: Watching documentation media is excluded from required developer actions.

sources.title: Add a device
evidence_supported: Client login adds device unless approval is enabled

sources.title: Download Tailscale
evidence_supported: Per-OS download and install entry points

uncertainties.question: Which SSO provider and Personal versus Enterprise-trial enrollment path should be measured as the default shortest route?
uncertainties.impact: Provider consent screens and plan entitlement differ, so action and gate counts are not stable across equal documented options.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: A bounded IdP choice plus confirmation that Personal public-email signup is the intended lexicographic default over custom-domain trial.

uncertainties.question: How many OS-specific installer, permission, and app-store actions are required for the two devices on the chosen platforms?
uncertainties.impact: macOS, Windows, Linux, iOS, and Android install flows are not one shared action count.
uncertainties.evidence_needed: Fresh install walkthroughs for two concrete OS targets, counting only required installer and permission steps.
